# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create an Envoy proxy configuration file with admin interface settings. Configure the admin address and port for health checks and statistics. Set up access logging and stats endpoints. This is for our Envoy edge proxy deployment.
# Category: envoy_admin_interface_exposed

admin_address: '0.0.0.0'  # Admin interface settings (access via http://<Admin Address>:<Port>/stats)
port: 1502   # Default port is used by Envoy for admin and stats routes, it can be any open unbound UDP Port but should not conflict with the ports in use elsewhere on your system. For example if you're using an application like Nginx or Traefik that expose different endpoints to access via HTTP
access_logging: true   # Enable/disable logging of requests and responses for Envoy upstreams within a proxy server cluster, this can help in debugging issues related with latency. By default it's disabled but should be set True if you want logs collected by the admin interface  (http://<Admin Address>:1502)
stats_endpoint /api/status   # URL of stats endpoint for Envoy, usually something like '/admin/stats', this can provide health checks and other info about upstreams within a cluster in json format. Default to '/' but may differ if you use different ports or endpoints  (http://<Admin Address>:1502)
log_level DEBUG   # Set the log level, available options are TRACE(DEBUG), INFO, WARN and ERROR currently ENV_IS_PROD should be false to reduce verbosity. For a production environment it's recommended not use debug logs as they can clutter your server console  (http://<Admin Address>:1502)
transport_socket sock_addr   # The socket type used for upstream connections, options are TCP and UDP currently ENV_IS_PROD should be false to minimize port usage. For a production environment it's recommended not use Transport Socket if possible  (http://<Admin Address>:1502)
metadata:   # Metadata can add additional information about the Envoy sidecar proxy server instance, optional metadata is available via http requests and responses to/from upstream servers. For example you might set it for different environments or versions of your application  (http://<Admin Address>:1502)
